stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key AI/ML topics including transformers, llama, and text-generation.
The metadata suggests the model is a significant, though perhaps not groundbreaking, release within the current LLM ecosystem.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under key AI/ML domains including transformers and text-generation.
The model is associated with Meta (implied by 'meta-llama') and is available via standard ML formats like safetensors.

stories.title: Microsoft discovers new lightweight backdoor that steals cryptocurrency
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: A new, lightweight backdoor named 'Crypto Clipper' has been discovered.
The malware is capable of stealing cryptocurrency from infected systems.
It spreads via USB drives and utilizes the Tor network for communication.

stories.title: Actionable Activation Directions for Detecting and Mitigating Emergent Misalignment Across Language Model Families
stories.author: Abdul Rafay Syed
bullet_points: Identifies that fine-tuning LLMs on insecure code causes emergent misalignment due to poorly understood internal structure.
Proposes and tests a 'causally actionable activation-space direction' to detect this misalignment across multiple model families.
Achieves extremely high separation (99.6%) between aligned and misaligned activations using a difference-in-means direction at the final layer.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, DeepSeek-R1, on the Hugging Face platform.
The model is categorized for text generation and utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors.
It falls under the 'deepseek_v3' family, indicating relevance within the competitive LLM landscape.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key ML/NLP technologies like transformers and PyTorch.
The context suggests tracking model performance and trends within the AI community.

stories.title: Ollama's highest performance on Apple Silicon yet with MLX
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has achieved its peak performance on Apple Silicon.
The performance boost is attributed to updates in the MLX engine.
Improvements include higher quality model outputs, faster response times, and reduced memory usage.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, now integrates MLX for enhanced performance.
This update specifically targets and optimizes for Apple Silicon hardware.
The feature is currently available in a preview release, indicating ongoing development.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring all processing runs directly on the user's device.
The core value proposition is providing private, on-device AI capabilities for coding assistance.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily setting up and running coding tools.
It supports various models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex, locally or in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: FDA advisors unanimously vote to approve Moderna's mRNA after agency drama
stories.author: Beth Mole
bullet_points: FDA advisors unanimously approved Moderna's mRNA vaccine.
The approval followed a period of administrative controversy, including a refusal by a Trump official to review the vaccine.
This marks a significant regulatory milestone for mRNA technology in vaccine development.

stories.title: As China looms, Taiwan makes more drones for defense and the US military
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: Taiwan is increasing its drone production for national defense.
The increased drone spending is also expected to benefit Taiwanese businesses internationally.
The context of escalating tensions with China is driving this defense buildup.

stories.title: Android verification is coming: Google confirms timeline and supported app stores
stories.author: Ryan Whitwam
bullet_points: Google is implementing a new Android verification system service this month.
Significant changes related to this verification are scheduled to begin in September.
The update will address and manage support for various app stores on Android.

stories.title: FlowEdit: Associative Memory for Lifelong Pronunciation Adaptation in Flow-Matching TTS
stories.author: Nityanand Mathur
bullet_points: FlowEdit addresses the static nature of state-of-the-art flow-matching Text-to-Speech (TTS) systems.
It enables lifelong pronunciation adaptation for frozen TTS models without requiring full retraining.
Adaptation is achieved by learning pronunciation corrections as latent conditioning edits in the text embedding space.

stories.title: StylisticBias: A Few Human Visual Cues Drive Most Social Biases in MLLMs
stories.author: Jana Diesner
bullet_points: Introduces 'StylisticBias,' a new benchmark to evaluate attribute-level social bias in Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs).
Addresses a critical gap in MLLM research by isolating appearance effects from inherent identity differences when judging people.
Highlights the growing societal risk associated with poorly understood visual cues influencing consequential AI deployments.

stories.title: Your Mouse and Eyes Secretly Leak Your Preference: LLM Alignment using Implicit Feedback from Users
stories.author: Hamed Zamani
bullet_points: The paper addresses the high cost and scarcity of explicit human feedback used in current LLM alignment methods.
It proposes using implicit user feedback (like mouse movements or gaze) to train LLM reward models.
This approach aims to leverage rich, passive user data, similar to how internet giants build their economic advantages.

stories.title: I found 10k GitHub repositories distributing Trojan malware
stories.author: theorchid
bullet_points: A significant security vulnerability was discovered involving Trojan malware.
The malware was found distributed across approximately 10,000 GitHub repositories.
This highlights a major risk in open-source software supply chains.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release brings performance improvements and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across various hardware platforms.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine, specifically improving support on Apple silicon.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA has introduced Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically engineered for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is designed to support complex, long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, providing an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The key feature is that these agents can run locally on the user's own hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating the use of local LLMs.

stories.title: Snap’s Specs look good on nobody
stories.author: David Pierce
bullet_points: Snap has released new smart glasses featuring advanced face-computer technology.
The glasses are designed to be relatively compact, avoiding the bulk of traditional VR headsets.
The high price point ($2,195) is noted, despite the device's impressive features.

stories.title: NASA asks Northrop Grumman to stop working on lunar HALO module
stories.author: Eric Berger
bullet_points: NASA has directed Northrop Grumman to cease work on the lunar HALO module.
The decision involves reassigning the majority of affected employees to other ongoing programs.
This represents a significant, albeit internal, shift in NASA's lunar exploration hardware development.

stories.title: How Transparent is DiffusionGemma?
stories.author: Neel Nanda
bullet_points: The research investigates the transparency of DiffusionGemma, an LLM utilizing a continuous latent space for computation.
Model reasoning transparency is highlighted as crucial for debugging, mitigating misuse, and ensuring alignment.
Transparency is decomposed into 'variable transparency' (understanding intermediate states) and algorithmic aspects.

stories.title: How Do Instructions Shape Speech? Cross-Attention Attribution for Style-Captioned Text-to-Speech
stories.author: Sudarshan Kamath
bullet_points: The research addresses the lack of clarity regarding how individual words influence the acoustic output in style-captioned Text-to-Speech (TTS) systems.
The authors propose adapting the DAAM framework to speech diffusion models to perform cross-attention attribution.
The method generates per-token heatmaps across multiple layers to improve the diagnosis of failure modes and controllability in expressive TTS.

stories.title: LedgerAgent: Structured State for Policy-Adherent Tool-Calling Agents
stories.author: Chitta Baral
bullet_points: Introduces LedgerAgent, a framework designed to give policy-adherent agents a structured state representation.
Addresses a key limitation in standard agents where task states (facts, constraints, conditions) are implicitly reconstructed from the prompt across turns.
Focuses on improving reliability in complex domains like customer service by explicitly managing state during tool-calling.

stories.title: DeepSWIP: Quotient-WMC Counterfactuals for Neural Probabilistic Logic Programs
stories.author: Fengxiang He
bullet_points: Introduces DeepSWIP, a new framework for counterfactual reasoning in neurosymbolic systems like DeepProbLog.
Addresses the limitation of standard associational inference by providing a single-world counterfactual semantics.
Achieves this by using neural materialization to translate neural predicates into standard ProbLog choices and applying SWIPs.

stories.title: SARLO-80: Worldwide Slant SAR Language Optic Dataset 80cm
stories.author: Georgia Channing
bullet_points: SARLO-80 introduces a new, large-scale dataset for combining Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) and optical imagery.
The dataset addresses a critical gap in multimodal AI by providing high-resolution SAR data that preserves complex-valued measurements and native acquisition geometry.
This resource aims to enable more physically grounded and advanced multimodal learning for SAR applications, overcoming limitations of existing low-resolution datasets.

stories.title: Sovereign Execution Brokers: Enforcing Certificate-Bound Authority in Agentic Control Planes
stories.author: Deying Yu
bullet_points: Addresses the security gap where autonomous agents' non-deterministic reasoning processes could compromise production mutation authority.
Proposes the Sovereign Execution Broker (SEB) as a runtime enforcement boundary for certified actions.
Distinguishes itself from existing identity-based access control and assurance layers by providing mandatory enforcement at the moment of mutation.

stories.title: Multi-LCB: Extending LiveCodeBench to Multiple Programming Languages
stories.author: Dmitrii Babaev
bullet_points: The paper introduces Multi-LCB, an extension of the LiveCodeBench (LCB) benchmark.
LCB is a recognized tool for evaluating LLMs on code generation, currently limited to Python.
Multi-LCB aims to address this limitation by extending the benchmark to multiple programming languages to test LLM generalization.

stories.title: How Transparent is DiffusionGemma?
stories.author: Neel Nanda
bullet_points: The paper investigates the transparency of DiffusionGemma, an LLM utilizing a continuous latent space for computation.
Model reasoning transparency is identified as crucial for debugging, mitigating misuse, and ensuring alignment in LLMs.
Transparency is decomposed into 'variable transparency' (understanding intermediate states) and algorithmic aspects.

stories.title: Predictability as a Fine-Grained Measure for Privacy
stories.author: Karthik Sridharan
bullet_points: Introduces 'privacy via predictability' as a new, fine-grained privacy framework.
Addresses the limitations of traditional Differential Privacy (DP) by moving beyond worst-case guarantees.
Explicitly incorporates attacker knowledge, compromised data portions, and query families into the privacy measure.

stories.title: Execution-State Capsules: Graph-Bound Execution-State Checkpoint and Restore for Low-Latency, Small-Batch, On-Device Physical-AI Serving
stories.author: Liang Su
bullet_points: Addresses a gap in LLM serving systems by focusing on low-latency, small-batch, on-device scenarios, contrasting with traditional high-throughput KV caching.
Introduces 'Execution-State Capsules' for checkpointing and restoring the full execution state, not just the KV cache.
Targets use cases like interactive LLM agents, speech systems, and robot policies that require frequent branching and tight responsiveness budgets.

stories.title: Probe-and-Refine Tuning of Repository Guidance for Coding Agents
stories.author: Jeannie Albrecht
bullet_points: The research addresses a critical gap in LLM coding agents: the lack of high-level operational knowledge about a software repository.
The paper investigates the effectiveness of using repository guidance (like AGENTS.md files) to improve agent performance.
It proposes a 'Probe-and-Refine Tuning' method to better integrate this necessary contextual knowledge into coding agents.

stories.title: LedgerAgent: Structured State for Policy-Adherent Tool-Calling Agents
stories.author: Chitta Baral
bullet_points: Introduces LedgerAgent, a framework designed to give policy-adherent agents a structured state for tool-calling.
Addresses a limitation in standard agents where task states (facts, constraints, conditions) must be reconstructed from the prompt each turn.
Focuses on improving agent reliability in complex domains like customer service by explicitly managing task state across interactions.

stories.title: Scalable Training of Spatially Grounded 2D Vision-Language Models for Radiology
stories.author: Thomas Brox
bullet_points: Introduces RefRad2D, a large-scale bilingual (German/English) dataset of 1.2M CT/MR image-text pairs for radiology.
Proposes RefRad2D to train spatially grounded Vision-Language Models (VLMs) without requiring manual spatial annotations.
The model, RadGrounder, performs joint tasks including report generation, visual question answering, and spatial grounding.

stories.title: CATCH-ME if you RAG: a dataset of Contextually Annotated multi-Turn Counterspeech against Hate and Misinformation Exchanges
stories.author: Marco Guerini
bullet_points: Addresses the overlap between hate speech and misinformation, a gap in current NLP research.
Proposes a new dataset ('CATCH-ME') to provide high-quality, contextually annotated examples for generating counterspeech.
Aims to improve LLM performance in generating non-repetitive and effective responses against harmful online exchanges.

stories.title: Token-Operations-Oriented Inference Optimization Techniques for Large Models
stories.author: Qinghuai Ma
bullet_points: The paper focuses on token-oriented inference optimization, a critical area for scalable and low-cost large model services.
It introduces a novel four-layer technical architecture: Multi-model Fusion, Model Optimization, Compute-Model Fusion, and Compute-Network-Model Fusion.
The work provides a systematic review of current key technologies and industry status across these optimization levels.

stories.title: PsyScore: A Psychometrically-Aware Framework for Trait-Adaptive Essay Scoring and ZPD-Scaffolded Feedback
stories.author: Chanjin Zheng
bullet_points: Introduces PsyScore, a framework designed to integrate psychometric principles into Automated Essay Scoring (AES).
Addresses a key limitation in current AES systems where scoring and instructional feedback are treated separately.
Aims to provide both reliable assessment and learner-specific, actionable feedback by being sensitive to proficiency levels.

stories.title: The Register Gap: A Meaning Intelligence Framework for Nigerian Public Discourse
stories.author: Celestine Achi
bullet_points: Introduces the Meaning Intelligence Framework (MIF) for analyzing Nigerian public discourse.
MIF is a nine-dimension schema designed to differentiate true communicative intent from surface sentiment.
Argues that AI failure in Nigerian discourse stems from context failure, not just translation issues, challenging existing sentiment benchmarks.

stories.title: To study how chips work, MIT researchers built their own operating system
stories.author: speckx
bullet_points: MIT researchers developed a custom operating system.
The OS was built specifically for the purpose of studying chip functionality.
This represents a deep dive into hardware-software interaction at a fundamental level.

stories.title: Why Your AI Model's Confidence Score Is Probably Lying (And What To Do About It)
stories.author: saeebarve
bullet_points: Addresses the critical issue of distribution shift in production AI models.
Focuses on the unreliability of AI model confidence scores.
Provides actionable advice for developers to mitigate these issues.
